question: A young artist from a northern Italian city began his training at age nine under a well-known studio master, who also mentored another future painter in his workshop. Later, this young artist gained the patronage of a religious leader who served in his highest office during the early 17th century and was known for both his involvement in scientific debates and for expanding the water distribution system of a historic European capital. What major infrastructure project did this religious leader commission atop a prominent hill west of the city center?
